explanation.rule: The suffix -ly is added to adjectives to form adverbs that describe how, when, or to what degree something happens.

groups.note: Added to adjectives to form adverbs. Adjectives ending in -e usually just add -ly (fortunate → fortunately); adjectives ending in -l also add -ly (final → finally).

examples.definition: at a fast speed; without much delay
in a way that is easy to understand or see
in a way that happens quickly and without warning
luckily; in a way that is good or favourable
at the end; after a long time or series of events

wordForms.definition: at a fast speed; without much delay
wordForms.example: She quickly finished her homework before dinner.
wordForms.rootDefinition: moving or happening at a fast speed

wordForms.definition: not quickly; at a low speed
wordForms.example: The traffic moved slowly through the city centre.
Read the instructions carefully before assembling the furniture.
wordForms.rootDefinition: giving attention to avoid mistakes or danger

wordForms.definition: in a way that is easy to understand or see
wordForms.example: The teacher explained the rules clearly to the whole class.
wordForms.rootDefinition: easy to see, hear, or understand

wordForms.definition: with great force or intensity; firmly
wordForms.example: I strongly believe that education is the key to success.
wordForms.rootDefinition: having great physical or mental power

wordForms.definition: in a way that happens quickly and without warning
wordForms.example: The car suddenly stopped in the middle of the road.
wordForms.rootDefinition: happening or done quickly and unexpectedly

wordForms.definition: luckily; in a way that is good or favourable
wordForms.example: It started to rain, but fortunately she had an umbrella.

wordForms.definition: not long ago; a short time before the present
wordForms.example: She recently moved to a new city for work.

wordForms.definition: in the way that happens most of the time; normally
wordForms.example: I usually wake up at seven o'clock on weekdays.
wordForms.rootDefinition: happening regularly or in the normal way

wordForms.definition: at the end; after a long time or series of events
wordForms.example: After months of searching, she finally found a flat.
wordForms.rootDefinition: coming at the end; last in a sequence

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: He spoke so ___ that everyone in the room could understand him.
The dog ___ ran out into the street when the door opened.
It started to rain, but ___, they had already finished the match.
Please read the contract ___ before you sign it.
After months of delays, the project was ___ completed.
I ___ drink coffee in the morning, but today I had tea.

transformExercises.sentence: He completed the task ___ and moved on to the next one. (QUICK)
It started raining, but ___, the rescue team had already arrived. (FORTUNATE)
I have ___ started learning a new language online. (RECENT)
She ___ expressed her opinion about the new policy at the meeting. (STRONG)
After three hours of waiting, the bus ___ arrived. (FINAL)
